articles.title: What makes a ‘heat dome’ and what does it mean?
articles.description:
Heat domes can make already-high temperatures even more extreme and prolonged, and they are worsening in severity and becoming more frequent as the planet warms.

The post What makes a ‘heat dome’ and what does it mean? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Heat domes are causing prolonged extreme temperatures in Boston.
Frequency of heat domes is increasing due to climate change.
Residents may face health risks and energy challenges during heat waves.

articles.title: Suspect flees after crowd throws drinks at lone BPD officer, prompting renewed staffing worries
articles.description:
Police worked to clear a chaotic, gridlocked intersection in Dorchester Sunday night.

The post Suspect flees after crowd throws drinks at lone BPD officer, prompting renewed staffing worries appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Crowd threw drinks at a BPD officer in Dorchester, causing chaos on Sunday night.
Officer was alone while managing a gridlocked intersection, raising staffing concerns.
Incident highlights ongoing issues with police presence and safety in Boston neighborhoods.

articles.title: What you need to know about Boston Harborfest
articles.description:
The Independence Day celebration takes place July 2-4.

The post What you need to know about Boston Harborfest appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston Harborfest celebrates Independence Day from July 2-4, 2023.
Expect over 200 events, including historical reenactments and concerts.
Local businesses anticipate increased foot traffic and sales during the holiday weekend.

articles.title: WCVB reshuffles weather lineup with Mike Wankum’s retirement
articles.description:
The changes are going into effect this week.

The post WCVB reshuffles weather lineup with Mike Wankum’s retirement appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: WCVB announces weather lineup changes following Mike Wankum's retirement effective this week.
Mike Wankum served as a meteorologist for over 30 years at WCVB.
Local viewers will see new faces in weather reporting, impacting daily forecasts.

articles.title: Boston-area restaurant chain takes Storrowing as opportunity to apologize, and plug their pizza
articles.description:
"Come enjoy one of our jumbo slices. It would definitely make today a little bit better for us!"

The post Boston-area restaurant chain takes Storrowing as opportunity to apologize, and plug their pizza appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston-area restaurant chain uses Storrowing incident to promote their jumbo pizza slices.
The chain issued an apology on social media, inviting locals to enjoy their pizza.
This marketing move aims to boost customer engagement following recent traffic disruptions.

articles.title: As heat wave looms, more beaches close due to sewage spill
articles.description:
As the sewage spill in Haverhill worsens, more downstream beaches are closing due to unsafe bacteria.

The post As heat wave looms, more beaches close due to sewage spill appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Haverhill sewage spill leads to beach closures along the Merrimack River.
Five beaches downstream are now closed due to unsafe bacteria levels.
Residents face limited recreational options during the upcoming heat wave.

articles.title: Supreme Court upholds state laws banning transgender girls and women from school athletic teams
articles.description:
More than two dozen other Republican-led states have adopted bans on female transgender athletes, and the decision seems certain to extend to them as well.

The post Supreme Court upholds state laws banning transgender girls and women from school athletic teams appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Supreme Court upholds bans on transgender girls in school sports as of June 30, 2026.
Over two dozen Republican-led states, including Massachusetts, may adopt similar bans.
Boston schools could see changes in athletic policies affecting transgender female students.
